results.rendered_html:
upa-√labh Ā. -labhate, to seize, get possession of, acquire, receive, obtain, find,  MBh. ;  R. ;  Mn. ;  Mṛcch.  &c.;
(with garbham) to conceive, become pregnant,  R. ;
to perceive, behold, hear [cf. Gk. ὑπολαμβάνω], to understand, learn, know, ascertain,  MBh. ;  BhP. ;  Pañcat. ;  VarBṛS.  &c.:
Caus. P. -lambhayati, to cause to obtain or take possession,  BhP. viii, 15, 36 ;
to cause to hear or learn or know,  Pat.  on Vārtt. 2 on  Pāṇ. i, 4, 52 ;
to cause to be known or distinguished,  BhP. iv, 1, 25  :
Desid. (p. -lipsamāna,  AV. vi, 118, 11 ) to wish to catch or grasp.

results.rendered_html:
upalabh 1 A.
1 To know, understand, see, perceive (directly);  Pt. 1. 76 ; anyā pratikriyā nopalabhyate  K. 152. 
2 To ascertain, find out, learn, know; brūhi yadupalabdhaṃ  U. 1 ; tattvata enāmupalapsye  Ś. 1 ;  Mv. 7. 30 ;  R. 12. 60 ;  Bk. 3. 27 ;  K. 159 ;  M. 4 , 3. 6 , 4. 6. 
3 To get, obtain, acquire, enjoy, experience (happiness &c.); upalabdhasukhaḥ  Ku. 4. 42  experiencing; kathamupalabhe nidrāṃ  V. 2. 10  enjoy; idānīṃ smṛtirupalabdhā  Mu. 2  now I recollect;  Ms. 11. 17 ;  R. 8. 82 , 10. 2 , 18. 21 .
4 To conceive, become pregnant.
— Caus.
1 To cause to get.
2 To produce, bring about.
